rationale: Both candidates correctly identify the actual thread resolution: the test was run at the default core_model run-level, which forces dummy weights, and the correct fix is --run-level=full_model with the issue closed as invalid — matching akshatvishu/yenuo26's actual resolution. Y is more thorough and technically precise, correctly noting the subtle point that the full_model pytest marker doesn't set the CLI run-level (so selecting by node ID silently falls back to core_model/dummy weights), and grounds the explanation across more specific files/lines (run_args.py, runtime.py, stage_config.py, assertions.py) plus addresses the reporter's A100 hardware question and practical prerequisites (weights, video path, GPU memory) more completely. X is also correct and concise but leans on a speculative, thread-unsupported aside about per-stage OOM tuning for A100. Y's invented specific Welsh audio transcript is an unverifiable embellishment, slightly denting its grounding, but its overall diagnosis and code-path explanation is marginally deeper and more complete than X's.
